evals.prompt:
I have a Fumadocs (MDX) site and need to port this GitHub-flavored markdown into valid MDX. Convert it, keeping the same content but using the correct MDX equivalents:

```md
# Setup Guide

> [!WARNING]
> This requires Node 20+.

> [!NOTE]
> See the [config reference](../reference/config.md) for all options.

<details>
<summary>Advanced: Custom auth provider</summary>

You can pass a custom provider:

```ts
const auth = createAuth({ provider: 'custom' });
```

</details>

Here is a footnote about our API limits.[^1]

[^1]: Rate limited to 100 req/min per API key.
```

The MDX site uses `fumadocs-ui` components: `<Callout type="warn">`, `<Callout type="info">`, and `<Accordions>`/`<Accordion>` for collapsibles. Imports go at the top of the file after frontmatter.
evals.expected_output: Valid MDX file with fumadocs-ui component imports at the top, GitHub callouts converted to <Callout> components with correct type props, <details>/<summary> converted to <Accordions>/<Accordion>, the .md relative link updated to .mdx or a valid path, and footnotes either preserved or converted to inline notes since MDX footnote support varies.

assertions.text: The output includes import statements for Callout and Accordion/Accordions components from fumadocs-ui at the top of the file (after any frontmatter).
GitHub blockquote callouts (> [!WARNING] and > [!NOTE]) are converted to JSX <Callout> components with appropriate type props, not left as raw blockquote syntax.
The <details>/<summary> HTML block is converted to <Accordions>/<Accordion> components from fumadocs-ui, not left as raw HTML.
The relative link ../reference/config.md is updated to a valid MDX-compatible path (e.g., ../reference/config or .mdx extension), not left pointing to a .md file.
The code block inside the accordion/collapsible is properly escaped or nested so it would not break JSX parsing (no unescaped curly braces in prose, code blocks are intact).

evals.prompt:
Write a CHANGELOG.md entry for version 2.4.0 of a library called 'dataforge'. The changes are:
- Breaking: renamed `DataForge.create()` to `DataForge.init()` and the old name is removed
- Added streaming support via new `stream()` method on query results
- Fixed: nested object serialization was dropping null values (issue #847)
- Fixed: connection pool exhaustion under concurrent writes (issue #901)
- Deprecated: `DataForge.connect()` in favor of `DataForge.init()` with connection options

Use the Keep a Changelog format (keepachangelog.com). Include diff fences showing the migration path for the breaking change. Link issues to https://github.com/example/dataforge/issues/.
evals.expected_output: A CHANGELOG entry following Keep a Changelog format with proper sections (Added, Changed/Breaking, Deprecated, Fixed), diff fences showing old vs new API, and linked issue references using markdown link syntax.

assertions.text: The entry organizes changes under Keep a Changelog section headers — specifically using separate sections for breaking changes, additions, fixes, and deprecations (e.g., Changed, Added, Fixed, Deprecated or similar).
The entry includes a diff-fenced code block (```diff) showing the migration from DataForge.create() to DataForge.init() with - and + prefixed lines.
Issue numbers #847 and #901 are linked as clickable markdown links pointing to https://github.com/example/dataforge/issues/847 and /901 respectively, not just plain text '#847'.
The version heading includes both the version number (2.4.0) and a date in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD), following Keep a Changelog convention.

evals.prompt:
I have a monorepo with this structure:

```
packages/
  core/
    README.md
    src/
      index.ts
  cli/
    README.md
    src/
      commands/
        deploy.ts
  docs/
    pages/
      getting-started.mdx
      api/
        core.mdx
        cli.mdx
```

Write the content for `packages/cli/README.md`. It should:
1. Link to the core package README using a correct relative path
2. Link to the docs site pages using relative paths that resolve from the cli package
3. Include a nested collapsible section: outer collapsible 'Platform Notes' containing two inner collapsibles 'macOS' and 'Linux', each with a fenced code block showing install commands
4. Use GitHub-flavored markdown throughout (callouts, task lists)
5. Include a mermaid sequence diagram showing: User -> CLI -> Core -> API
evals.expected_output: A CLI README with correct relative links to ../core/README.md and ../docs/pages/*.mdx, properly nested <details> blocks that won't break GitHub rendering, a mermaid sequence diagram, GitHub callouts, and task lists.

assertions.text: The README links to the core package using the correct relative path ../core/README.md (not an absolute path, not ./core, not /packages/core).
The README links to at least one docs page using a relative path that correctly traverses from packages/cli/ to packages/docs/pages/ (e.g., ../docs/pages/getting-started.mdx).

The output contains nested collapsible sections — an outer <details> block containing at least two inner <details> blocks, with blank lines separating the HTML tags from markdown content to prevent rendering issues.
The output includes a mermaid sequence diagram (```mermaid with sequenceDiagram keyword) showing interactions between User, CLI, Core, and API.
The output uses at least one GitHub-flavored callout (> [!NOTE], > [!TIP], > [!WARNING], etc.) not a generic blockquote.
